# Dependencies — Line 1g: Wages from Form 8919 line 6

**Tax year:** 2025
**Form:** 1040 / 1040-SR / 1040-NR line 1g
**Source-of-truth mapping:** `Form1040.Line1g = addNonNull(taxpayer.Form8919.Line6, spouse.Form8919.Line6)`

Line 1g carries WAGES that should have been treated as employee wages, but the firm did not withhold the employee share of Social Security and Medicare tax. The worker pays BOTH the income tax (line 1g → AGI) AND the employee FICA share (Schedule 2 line 6) the firm should have withheld but didn't. Form 8919 is required only when the worker performed services, believes the pay should have been W-2 wages, the firm did not withhold employee FICA, and one of reason codes A, C, G, or H applies.

---

## Outputs Produced

| Output | Return field | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `form1040.income.uncollectedSocialSecurityMedicareWages` | Form 1040 line 1g | `addNonNull(taxpayer.Form8919.line6, spouse.Form8919.line6)` |
| `schedule2.otherTaxes.uncollectedSocialSecurityMedicareTaxOnWages` | Schedule 2 line 6 | `addNonNull(taxpayer.Form8919.line13, spouse.Form8919.line13)` |
| `schedule2.otherTaxes.totalAdditionalSocialSecurityMedicareTax` | Schedule 2 line 7 subtotal | `line5 + line6` |
| `form8919Taxpayer` | Full Form 8919 output (taxpayer) | Lines 1–13; firm rows; null when no taxpayer firms |
| `form8919Spouse` | Full Form 8919 output (spouse) | Lines 1–13; firm rows; null when no spouse firms OR on MFS |
| `form8959.line3UncollectedWages` (effective) | Form 8959 Part I line 3 | Form 8919 line 6 participates in Additional Medicare Tax base |

Wired in `buildIncome()` at line 7361 (line 1z aggregation).

---

## Upstream Computed Dependency

`computeUncollectedSSTax()` consumes the `TipComputation` result. **`computeTips()` MUST run first** so that `form4137Taxpayer.line10` / `form4137Spouse.line10` (unreported tips subject to Social Security) are available for Form 8919 line 8.

Execution order (enforced in `prepare()`):

```
computeTips()                          → produces form4137Taxpayer, form4137Spouse
computeUncollectedSSTax(tips, ...)     → reads form4137.line10 for Form 8919 line 8
```

---

## Constants (2025)

| Constant | Value | Statutory basis |
|---|---|---|
| `SOCIAL_SECURITY_WAGE_BASE` | $176,100 | SSA 2025 announcement; Form 8919 line 7 |
| SS tax rate | 6.2% | IRC §3101(a); inline at lines ~20351-20352 |
| Medicare rate | 1.45% | IRC §3101(b)(1); no wage-base cap |
| `ADDITIONAL_MEDICARE_THRESHOLD_SINGLE/HOH/QSS` | $200,000 | IRC §3101(b)(2) |
| `ADDITIONAL_MEDICARE_THRESHOLD_MFJ` | $250,000 | IRC §3101(b)(2) |
| `ADDITIONAL_MEDICARE_THRESHOLD_MFS` | $125,000 | IRC §3101(b)(2) |
| `ADDITIONAL_MEDICARE_RATE` | 0.9% | IRC §3101(b)(2) |

The 6.2% and 1.45% rates are hard-coded inline at lines ~20351-20352, NOT in `ReferenceData`.

---

## Per-Spouse Form 8919 Computation

```
line6  = Σ parseAmount(firm.wagesNoFicaNotW2)     (column f; via addNonNull, skipping null rows)
line7  = $176,100                                  (2025 SS wage base)
line8  = sumW2Box3SsWagesForSsn
       + sumW2Box7SsTipsForSsn
       + sumW2Box14RrtaForSsn
       + form4137.line10                           (unreported tips subject to SS)
line9  = max(0, line7 − line8)                     (remaining SS wage base)
line10 = min(line6, line9)                         (wages subject to SS tax)
line11 = line10 × 0.062                            (6.2% employee SS tax)
line12 = line6 × 0.0145                            (1.45% employee Medicare; no cap)
line13 = line11 + line12                           (per-person total uncollected tax)
```

All lines whole-dollar HALF_UP rounded via `roundMoney`.

---

## Return-Level Aggregation

```
form1040.income.uncollectedSocialSecurityMedicareWages
    = addNonNull(taxpayer.line6, spouse.line6)

schedule2.otherTaxes.uncollectedSocialSecurityMedicareTaxOnWages
    = addNonNull(taxpayer.line13, spouse.line13)

Form8959.line3 = mfj
    ? addNonNull(taxpayer.line6, spouse.line6)
    : taxpayer.line6
```

On MFS: orchestrator passes `null` for spouse data at line 19442 → `computeForm8919ForPerson` early-returns empty.

---

## Per-Firm Inputs (columns a–f, lines 1–5)

| Field | Required | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `firmName` | Yes | Column (a) |
| `firmFederalIdNumber` | Yes (since 2026-05-19) | Column (b); literal `unknown` acceptable when EIN truly not known |
| `reasonCode` | Yes | Column (c); one of `A`, `C`, `G`, `H` (normalised: trim + upper) |
| `irsDeterminationDate` | Conditional | Column (d); required if code A or C |
| `received1099MiscOrNec` | No (boolean) | Column (e); contextual for code H |
| `wagesNoFicaNotW2` | Yes | Column (f); feeds line 6 |

---

## Reason Codes

| Code | Meaning | IRS date required? |
|---|---|---|
| `A` | IRS issued a determination letter saying the worker is an employee | YES (column d) |
| `C` | IRS issued other correspondence saying the worker is an employee | YES (column d) |
| `G` | Worker filed Form SS-8 and has not yet received a reply | n/a (SS-8 separate; out of scope) |
| `H` | Worker received both a W-2 and a 1099-MISC/NEC from the same firm for the same services | n/a (SS-8 should NOT be filed) |

---

## Input Statement Sources

| Statement | Field read | Use |
|---|---|---|
| `w-2` | `socialSecurityWagesAmount` (box 3) + `employeeSSN` | Sum into line 8 via `sumW2SocialSecurityWagesAndTipsForSsn`, SSN-filtered |
| `w-2` | `socialSecurityTipsAmount` (box 7) + `employeeSSN` | Sum into line 8, SSN-filtered |
| `w-2` | `box14Entries[]` RRTA tier-1 + `employeeSSN` | Sum into line 8 via `sumW2RrtaCompensationForSsn`, SSN-filtered |

Form 4137 line 10 is sourced from `computeTips()` (per-spouse), NOT directly from a statement.

---

## Input Personal Forms

| Form ID | Firestore path | Required | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `uncollected-ss-medicare-taxpayer` | `users/{uid}/uncollected-ss-medicare-taxpayer` | Conditional | Required when taxpayer has qualifying firms |
| `uncollected-ss-medicare-spouse` | `users/{uid}/uncollected-ss-medicare-spouse` | Conditional | Required when spouse has qualifying firms (MFJ only) |
| `you` | `users/{uid}/you` | Yes | `ssn` drives W-2 SSN attribution |
| `spouse` | `users/{uid}/spouse` | MFJ only | `ssn` drives spouse W-2 attribution |
| `filing-status` | `users/{uid}/filing-status` | Yes | `filingStatus` → `isMfsReturn` → spouse-side gate |

---

## Output to Forms

| Form | How it consumes Form 8919 |
|---|---|
| Form 8919 (PDF — taxpayer + spouse) | Up to 5 firm rows per page; `buildMultiPagePdf` chunks firms; lines 6–13 on first page only; overflow pages contain lines 1–5 only |
| Form 1040 line 1g | Receives `addNonNull(t.line6, s.line6)` |
| Schedule 2 line 6 | Receives `addNonNull(t.line13, s.line13)` |
| Schedule 2 line 7 | Sum `line5 + line6` |
| Form 1040 line 23 (other taxes) | Indirect via Schedule 2 total |
| Form 8959 line 3 | Form 8919 line 6 participates in Medicare wage base for 0.9% Additional Medicare Tax |

---

## Cross-Line Collision Protection

- **Line 1b household-employee guard**: `householdEmployeeAmount()` SKIPS employer rows where `reportedOnForm8919 == true` — prevents the same wages from being counted on both line 1b and line 1g
- **Line 1c Form 4137 tip collision**: NOT currently guarded — same wage row entered as both an unreported tip on Form 4137 AND a Form 8919 firm row would double-count (Code Validation #3; deferred advisory)
- **W-2 box 12 codes M/N (group-term life)**: NOT routed via Form 8919; belong on Schedule 2 line 5 (out of scope for line 1g)

---

## Validation Flags

| Flag | Trigger | Blocking |
|---|---|---|
| `FORM8919_REASON_CODE_INVALID_TAXPAYER` / `_SPOUSE` | Reason code missing or not in `{A, C, G, H}` | YES |
| `FORM8919_IRS_DATE_REQUIRED_TAXPAYER` / `_SPOUSE` | Code A or C with missing `irsDeterminationDate` | YES |
| `FORM8919_SS8_REQUIRED_TAXPAYER` / `_SPOUSE` | Code G (informational) — file Form SS-8 separately | NO |
| `FORM8919_CODE_H_1099_TAXPAYER` / `_SPOUSE` | Code H with `received1099MiscOrNec != true` | YES (rules.md §17 non-overrideable; promoted 2026-05-31). Code H requires both a W-2 AND a 1099-MISC/NEC from the same firm. Without the 1099, filing as-is invites near-certain IRS document-matching correspondence (CP-2057). User must switch to G/A/C or delete the row. |
| `MISSING_FIRM_FEDERAL_ID_FORM_8919_TAXPAYER` / `_SPOUSE` | Form 8919 created AND any firm row has missing/blank `firmFederalIdNumber` | YES (since 2026-05-19, per save-vs-compute principle). Compute is correct; flag prevents persistence of a Form 8919 that IRS e-file would reject. UI shows advisory hint but never refuses save. Literal `unknown` acceptable. |

Non-flag defensive behaviors (silent):
- `data == null` → `Form8919Result(null, null, null)`
- `firmEntries` empty → same silent return
- No `hasAnyForm8919RowInput` → silent return
- `wagesNoFicaNotW2` null for a firm → 0 contribution
- W-2 box 3/7 absent for spouse SSN → 0 line 8 contribution

---

## Compute Order

| Step | Operation |
|---|---|
| 1 | Load `uncollected-ss-medicare-taxpayer/spouse`, `you`, `spouse`, `filing-status` |
| 2 | Load W-2 statements |
| 3 | `computeTips()` → `TipComputation` with `form4137.line10UnreportedTipsSubjectSocialSecurity` per spouse |
| 4 | `computeUncollectedSSTax(uncollectedTaxpayer, uncollectedSpouse, you, spouse, w2Entries, tips, isMfsReturn, flags)` at line 413 (orchestrator at 19422; per-spouse helper `computeForm8919ForPerson` at 19454) |
| 5 | `buildIncome()` at line 7361 — `line1z = addNonNull(... line1g ...)` |
| 6 | Line 9 = `addNonNull(line1z, line2b, ...)` at line 7440 |
| 7 | Form 8959 computation reads `Form 8919 line 6` into `line3` |
| 8 | Schedule 2 line 6 + line 7 subtotals emitted |

---

## SS Wage Base Interaction (Medicare Has No Cap)

When W-2 SS wages (box 3 + box 7 + RRTA + Form 4137 line 10) already reach $176,100:

- `line9 = 0` → `line10 = 0` → `line11 = 0` (no SS tax owed on Form 8919 wages)
- `line12 = line6 × 1.45%` still applies (Medicare has no wage-base ceiling)
- `line13 = line12` only

---

## Key Invariants

- `Form 8919 line 6` is the WAGE portion (→ line 1g + → Form 8959 line 3)
- `Form 8919 line 13` is the TAX portion (→ Schedule 2 line 6)
- Form 8919 is PERSON-SPECIFIC; non-joint return = 0 or 1 form; MFJ = 0, 1, or 2 forms
- MFS gate at orchestrator (line 19442) passes `null` for spouse data → no spouse Form 8919
- Line 1g cannot be negative — wages are sums of user-entered amounts (each ≥ 0 by UI validation)
- Multi-page PDF: lines 6–13 on first page only; lines 1–5 fill overflow pages
- Line 8 contribution from Form 4137 line 10 enforces upstream order — `computeTips` MUST run first
- Line 1g feeds line 1z via `addNonNull` (signed pass-through, but always ≥ 0 in practice)

---

## Downstream Consumers

- Line 1g → line 1z → line 9 → AGI → line 11b → line 15
- Form 8919 line 13 → Schedule 2 line 6 → Schedule 2 total → Form 1040 line 23
- Form 8919 line 6 → Form 8959 line 3 → 0.9% Additional Medicare Tax when total Medicare wages > threshold
- Line 1g → EIC earned income (IRC §32 — Form 8919 wages qualify as wages per IRC §3121)
- Line 1g → Schedule 8812 ACTC earned income (line 18a)
- Line 1g → Form 2441 earned income (IRC §21)

---

## Out of Scope

- W-2 box 12 codes M/N (group-term life uncollected taxes) — Schedule 2 line 5
- Form SS-8 worker classification filing (separate IRS process; gated as code G advisory)
- True independent-contractor income (Schedule C / Schedule SE)
- Unreported tip income other than the Form 4137 line 10 contribution to Form 8919 line 8
- RRTA compensation in line 8 — spec-defined and backend reads W-2 box 14 RRTA, but no E2E test coverage
